title: Study Designer (AI)
description: The Study Designer uses AI to help you formulate observational study protocols. Describe your research question in plain language and the system will split it into a target population and outcome, then recommend validated phenotype definitions from the OHDSI Phenotype Library. It also includes a cohort definition linter to catch common design errors.
tips: Start on the Study Intent tab by describing your research question in natural language, such as 'Compare heart failure risk in patients on SGLT2 inhibitors vs DPP-4 inhibitors with type 2 diabetes.'
Click 'Analyze Study Intent' to have the AI split your description into a target population and an outcome.
Review the AI-ranked phenotype recommendations, which are scored by relevance to your stated intent.
Use the Phenotype Search tab to manually search the OHDSI library for specific conditions, drugs, or procedures.
The Cohort Lint tab accepts a cohort definition JSON and checks for issues like missing washout periods, empty concept sets, and inverted time windows.
Phenotype recommendations include a rationale explaining why each definition was suggested for your study.
Recommendations and search results display cohort IDs that you can use to look up full definitions in the Phenotype Library.
Use the linter before finalizing any cohort definition to catch design issues early in the study development process.
